_meta.title: C9 NCCL multi-rank binding — reconnaissance + resolution plan for CLM 7B FSDP blocker
_meta.author: reconnaissance-agent (opus-4.6-1m, 1M ctx)
_meta.purpose: Fact-map of what exists vs stub vs missing in the hexa→NCCL chain, with option tradeoff for unblocking CLM 7B FSDP launch.
ssot_refs: hexa-lang commits d0772a6 (C9 Day-1) + 65870fe (Linux duplicate-symbol fix)

current_state.summary: C9 NCCL is NOT fully stub at rc=99 as the launch checklist suggests. It is two-layer stub: nexus-side `training/nccl_ffi.hexa` (10KB, rc=99 for world>1) sits above hexa-lang `self/ml/hxccl.hexa` (7.9KB hexa + 16.5KB C) which has a complete Day-1 stub AND a prepared HXCCL_NCCL macro path for Day-2 real NCCL routing. The real work needed is Day-2 flip — wire NCCL calls inside the #ifdef HXCCL_NCCL branches (currently empty).

surface_family_a_fsdp.purpose: In-place AllGather/ReduceScatter against pre-sized recv buffer — consumed by fsdp.hexa / zero_shard.hexa in hexa-lang/self/ml/

surface_family_b_handle.purpose: Handle-based AllReduce/Broadcast surface matching NCCL signatures — for external training loops outside FSDP

build_status.linux_dup_symbol_fix: committed hexa-lang 65870fe (init split: Family A stateless vs Family B handle)
build_status.day2_nccl_real: stub — every ifdef HXCCL_NCCL block in hxccl_linux.c is currently empty commentary (lines 115-118, 153-157, 180-183, 197-199, 241-244, 275-278, 297-299, 310-313). Build script already supports `--with-nccl` + `-DHXCCL_NCCL=1 -lnccl -lcudart` (scripts/build_hxccl_linux.hexa:167-169).
build_status.uncommitted_changes_on_hxccl: NONE (hexa-lang git status shows hxccl.hexa + hxccl_linux.c + build_hxccl_linux.hexa clean)

hexa_lang_side_shim.mac_build: scripts/build_hxccl_linux.hexa --mac-stub produces libhxccl_stub.dylib symlinked to libhxccl.dylib; test_hxccl.hexa smoke Mac (T1 PASS + T2-T5 SKIP without HXCCL_FFI_LIVE=1)

sibling_session_activity.search_method: hexa-lang git log --since 2026-04-15 on self/ml/hxccl.hexa + self/native/hxccl_linux.c + scripts/build_hxccl_linux.hexa + self/test_hxccl.hexa
sibling_session_activity.active_sessions_on_c9: NONE currently — last C9 commit was 65870fe on 2026-04-16 (Linux duplicate-symbol fix). No session has hxccl files open in uncommitted state.
other_active_sessions_on_hexa_lang: B10/B11 work (parser perf + O(n²) purge — commits 54d486b, 56b80dd)
Metal FFI + M4 float stuff (1fcd639, be204ab) — unrelated
sibling_session_activity.rationale: No session is claiming Day-2 flip. Task owner must land it — either this session later (when H100 pod has 2 ranks) or a dedicated C9-Day2 session. The claim file `shared/state/c9_fsdp_native_20260416.json` marks C9 COMPLETED at 10:35Z 2026-04-16 (agent-a4176ea5, single-rank only).

extern_fns_already_present.hxccl_init / hxccl_init_handle: covers nccl_comm_init (both stateless and handle forms)
extern_fns_already_present.hxccl_broadcast_float: covers nccl_broadcast (float specialization, dtype expansion is Day-2 ABI slot)

blocker_assessment.verdict: All 5 task-spec extern fns map 1:1 to existing hxccl surface. The `stub rc=99` label in clm_7b_launch_checklist_20260417 refers to the nexus-side `training/nccl_ffi.hexa` wrapper, NOT to a missing extern surface in hexa-lang. The real Day-2 work is filling the `#ifdef HXCCL_NCCL` branches in hxccl_linux.c (8 branches × ~2-5 LoC each = ~30-50 LoC of real ncclAllReduce/ncclBroadcast/ncclCommInitRank calls), compiling with `--with-nccl`, and either (a) updating training/nccl_ffi.hexa to call hxccl_* instead of returning NCCL_TODO_RC=99, or (b) bypassing nccl_ffi.hexa and having fsdp_shard.hexa call hxccl directly.

option_a_bridge_existing_hxccl.description: Route nexus `training/nccl_ffi.hexa` multi-rank paths through hxccl.hexa externs. Add `@link("hxccl") extern fn hxccl_*` declarations, replace `return NCCL_TODO_RC` with real hxccl calls, keep the current fsdp_shard.hexa surface stable.
files_to_modify: training/nccl_ffi.hexa (~80 LoC edit: add 5 externs + replace 5 rc=99 returns with real calls)
option_a_bridge_existing_hxccl.risk: LOW — externs proven by hexa-lang test_hxccl.hexa smoke + fsdp.hexa reference. Only new code path is pack/unpack from nexus's layer-broadcast helper semantics (D, FF tensor dims) into hxccl's count+dtype flat-buffer semantics.
option_a_bridge_existing_hxccl.day2_c_work_done_by: hexa-lang Day-2 NCCL macro flip (separate task)
option_a_bridge_existing_hxccl.bypasses_silent_exit_risk: YES — nccl_ffi.hexa imports only 0 modules currently, this brings +1 (hxccl via @link) that is extern-only, not a `use` (the extern fn declarations don't trigger flatten_imports path), so no additional import-chain length.
option_a_bridge_existing_hxccl.allows_cross_ranks_on_separate_pods: YES via NCCL's rendezvous. Depends on MASTER_ADDR/MASTER_PORT env propagation — already in launch_command_sequence.launch_rank_0_pod_a line 200.

option_b_direct_hxccl_from_fsdp.description: Skip nexus's nccl_ffi.hexa entirely. fsdp_shard.hexa talks to hxccl directly via @link("hxccl") externs.
files_to_modify: training/fsdp_shard.hexa (~60 LoC edit: replace nccl_broadcast_layer/nccl_reduce_scatter_layer calls with hxccl_all_gather/hxccl_reduce_scatter)
training/nccl_ffi.hexa: DELETE (or leave for backward compat)
option_b_direct_hxccl_from_fsdp.risk: MEDIUM — refactoring the wrapper layer means the 34/34 single-rank tests in c9_fsdp_native_20260416 need to be re-run against the new path. Loss of nexus-local semantics (per-layer broadcast with D/FF dims → flat buffer count)
option_b_direct_hxccl_from_fsdp.pros: Removes a stub layer; closer to metal; simpler dependency graph
option_b_direct_hxccl_from_fsdp.cons: Violates the soft-abstraction that nccl_ffi.hexa was designed to offer; requires retesting

option_c_day2_nccl_macro_flip_upstream.description: Fill the 8 empty #ifdef HXCCL_NCCL branches in self/native/hxccl_linux.c with real ncclCommInitRank / ncclAllReduce / ncclBroadcast / ncclAllGather / ncclReduceScatter / ncclCommDestroy calls, rebuild with --with-nccl, deploy to H100 pod.
files_to_modify: hexa-lang/self/native/hxccl_linux.c (~50 LoC edit: 8 ifdef HXCCL_NCCL branches become real NCCL calls)
hexa-lang/self/ml/hxccl.hexa (NO edit — the hexa surface stays identical)
option_c_day2_nccl_macro_flip_upstream.risk: LOW once nccl.h present — the API is stable, signatures fixed, and the Day-1 stub already encodes correct (rank, world_size, count, dtype) → NCCL argument forwarding; only the C bodies need real calls
option_c_day2_nccl_macro_flip_upstream.pros: Single commit into hexa-lang repo, both pods benefit, all other FSDP consumers (fsdp.hexa, zero_shard.hexa, zero_optimizer.hexa, zero_plusplus.hexa) unblock in one shot
option_c_day2_nccl_macro_flip_upstream.cons: Requires H100 pod with CUDA-12.4 + NCCL-2.18+ to verify (cannot be fully validated on Mac)
dependency_libraries: nccl.h (from nccl-dev or CUDA bundle — already at /usr/include/nccl.h on runpod/pytorch:2.4.0)
libnccl.so (from libnccl2 apt package — ldconfig -p | grep libnccl verifies)

option_d_do_nothing_single_rank_fallback.description: Launch 7B single-rank (no FSDP), tolerate 80GB OOM risk, defer multi-rank to Day-2
option_d_do_nothing_single_rank_fallback.risk: HIGH — 7B Adam unsharded = 84GB > 80GB H100 SXM. Will OOM at step 1 or during first optimizer update
option_d_do_nothing_single_rank_fallback.verdict: NOT VIABLE for 7B; acceptable only for 3B fallback

recommendation.recommended: option_c FIRST (Day-2 macro flip) THEN option_a (bridge nccl_ffi.hexa)
recommendation.rationale: Option C is the structural fix — it lands real NCCL once in hexa-lang/self/native/hxccl_linux.c and unblocks every hexa consumer (nexus FSDP, hexa-lang self/ml/fsdp.hexa, zero_shard, zero_optimizer, zero_plusplus) simultaneously. Option A on top is a trivial shim to propagate it into the existing nexus wrapper surface without breaking fsdp_shard.hexa callers or the 34/34 test baseline. ETA: 6h (C) + 4h (hexa bridge) = 10h total, serial. Can be parallelized across 2 sessions to 6h wall.
recommendation.critical_path_impact: Option C+A lands on H100 pod 2026-04-17T15:00Z if started immediately. Matches optimistic_launch_utc in clm_7b_launch_checklist_20260417 line 40.
preflight_required_before_day2: Verify H100 pod has: ls /usr/local/cuda-12.4/include/cuda_runtime.h && ls /usr/include/nccl.h && ldconfig -p | grep libnccl
Check NCCL version >= 2.14 for ncclAvg (hxccl_linux.c line 277 comment): nccl-config --version
Confirm 2x H100 pods both have identical CUDA-12.4 + NCCL-2.18+ images
Run test_hxccl.hexa on pod with HXCCL_FFI_LIVE=1 to verify Day-1 stub paths; expected T2-T5 PASS on world=1

branches_to_fill.real_code_sketch: ncclUniqueId id; if (rank == 0) ncclGetUniqueId(&id); broadcast id via TCP rendezvous (MASTER_ADDR/MASTER_PORT); ncclCommInitRank(&g_hxccl_comm, world_size, id, rank);
ncclReduceScatter(sp, rp, (size_t)count, dtype_to_nccl(dtype), ncclSum, g_hxccl_comm, g_hxccl_stream);

ncclGroupStart(); float dummy=1.0f; float* d; cudaMalloc(&d, 4); cudaMemcpy(d, &dummy, 4, cudaMemcpyHostToDevice); ncclAllReduce(d, d, 1, ncclFloat, ncclSum, g_hxccl_comm, g_hxccl_stream); cudaStreamSynchronize(g_hxccl_stream); cudaFree(d); ncclGroupEnd();
Identical init to Family A but stores comm in the heap handle; Family A+B share a comm (set g_hxccl_comm = h->comm)
ncclAllReduce(bp, bp, count, ncclFloat, (op==0 ? ncclSum : ncclAvg), h->comm, h->stream);
ncclBroadcast(bp, bp, count, ncclFloat, root, h->comm, h->stream);

new_helpers_needed_c: static ncclDataType_t dtype_to_nccl(int64_t d) { switch(d){case 0:return ncclFloat32;case 1:return ncclFloat16;case 2:return ncclBfloat16;default:return ncclFloat32;}}
static int rendezvous_exchange_id(int rank, int world, ncclUniqueId* id) { /* TCP socket to MASTER_ADDR:MASTER_PORT, rank 0 sends, others recv */ }
implementation_details_option_c.smoke_test_multi_rank: On 2-rank pod: MASTER_ADDR=<ip> MASTER_PORT=29500 FSDP_WORLD_SIZE=2 FSDP_RANK=<r> HXCCL_FFI_LIVE=1 ./hexa run self/test_hxccl.hexa → expect 5 PASS both ranks, handle init returns comm, finalize closes cleanly

changes: Add `@link("hxccl") extern fn hxccl_init(rank, world_size) -> int` + 4 more (see hexa-lang/self/ml/hxccl.hexa:39-63 for exact signatures)
Replace `nccl_init` body: if world_size > 1, call hxccl_init_handle(rank, world_size) and stash handle in module-level global, set NCCL_WORLD_SIZE/RANK
Replace `nccl_barrier` body: if world > 1, call hxccl_barrier() (Family A uses globals) — RC forward

Replace `nccl_broadcast_layer` body: compute n = D*FF + D (layer tensor count) — call hxccl_broadcast_float(handle, buf, n, owner) — but needs per-tensor loop; alternative flatten all 8 tensors of a layer into one buf first
Replace `nccl_reduce_scatter_layer` body: call hxccl_reduce_scatter(send_buf, recv_buf, count, 0) — needs matching buf sizing
Replace `nccl_all_reduce_loss` body: call hxccl_allreduce_float(handle, one_elem_buf, 1, HXCCL_OP_AVG), unpack float
implementation_details_option_a.test_re_run: hexa run training/test_fsdp_shard.hexa — must still 34/34 PASS on Mac (single-rank path unchanged); + new test_fsdp_multi.hexa on pod for multi-rank

session_b_coordination.rationale: No hexa-lang session has hxccl.hexa / hxccl_linux.c / build_hxccl_linux.hexa in uncommitted state. Active sibling sessions are on stage0/parser/codegen (14/54d486b/56b80dd). No C9 claim active.
session_b_coordination.reason_blocking: 7B FSDP multi-rank is REQUIRED — single-rank OOMs at 84GB > 80GB H100. Cannot launch 7B without this landing. Option C is the path; can be done by this session on a separate branch or by a new dedicated C9-Day2 session.
session_b_coordination.handoff_note: If a sibling session picks up Option C while this session preps training, the `hexa-lang/self/native/hxccl_linux.c` becomes the sync point — this session's nexus bridge (Option A) can land first (independent of C side since it only references extern declarations whose signatures are frozen).

closed_loop_verification_plan.hxccl_mac_smoke: HXCCL_FFI_LIVE=1 hexa run self/test_hxccl.hexa → 5/0/0 PASS/FAIL/SKIP with Day-1 stub
closed_loop_verification_plan.hxccl_linux_day2_world_1: Day-2 libhxccl.so + HXCCL_FFI_LIVE=1 on H100 → 5/0/0 + nccl comm actually created (verify with NCCL_DEBUG=INFO)
closed_loop_verification_plan.hxccl_linux_day2_world_2: 2x H100 pod + MASTER_ADDR=pod_a_ip MASTER_PORT=29500 → rank 0/1 both reach barrier + allreduce semantic correct (sum of identical bufs = 2*buf)
closed_loop_verification_plan.fsdp_world_2_correctness: On 2x H100 toy model (12-layer tiny), train 10 steps, verify loss matches single-rank run within 1e-4 and cross-rank grad sync is exact
closed_loop_verification_plan.7b_launch_gate: After above PASS: fire 7B launch command from clm_7b_launch_checklist_20260417.launch_command_sequence
